items.summary: Wild horse numbers in the NSW Snowy Mountains are back up with a reproductive control trial now being considered as aerial shooting has been confirmed to resume.

items.title: Is it worth getting a robotic lawnmower?
items.summary: Experts say robot lawnmowers are becoming more accessible, but there are some key features to look out for.

items.title: Platypus population in Australia's oldest national park increases to at least 20
items.summary: There are at least 20 platypuses now living in the NSW Royal National Park's waterways, three years after their historic reintroduction.

items.title: Hydra, a female platypus, was among four released into the Royal National Park this week.
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Eleven Australian activists from Global Sumud Flotilla released
items.summary: Social Services Minister Tanya Plibersek has told ABC News Breakfast that the 11 Australians are now in Istanbul and are being assisted by Australian officials.

items.title: Queensland cricket club gives women a safe and inclusive place to play
items.summary: A Queensland cricket team, started by a mother who wanted to give her autistic daughter a safe space to play, has just embarked on an international tour.

items.title: Republican revolt over Donald Trump's 'weaponisation' fund stalls ICE funding vote
items.summary: US Senate Republicans have furious internal disagreements over a proposed compensation fund for Trump allies who claim they have been unfairly targeted by federal agencies.

items.title: Dermatologist drought in central Queensland leaves patients with few treatment options
items.summary: Regional Queensland is facing a shortage of dermatologists, leaving some patients increasingly desperate.

items.title: Farmers struggle to source cottonseed for cattle feed amid dry conditions
items.summary: A by-product of the cotton harvest, cottonseed boosts protein and fibre in animal diets. But as graziers ramp up their drought preparations, dry conditions are causing a supply slowdown.

items.title: Murray-Darling inflow laws secure water for delicate ecosystems, ecologist says
items.summary: Controversial new legislation could secure critical water supplies for delicate ecosystems while delivering benefits to the Murray-Darling Basin's agricultural sector, a leading river ecologist says.

items.title: Almost 1.2 billion people living with mental disorders worldwide as case numbers nearly double since 1990
items.summary: A surge over the last three decades means mental disorders now account for more than 17 per cent of all disability worldwide, according to a report published this week.

items.title: What is 'pink-slime' journalism and has it infiltrated Australian media?
items.summary: Experts say a series of AI-generated digital news outlets is the first recorded example of "pink-slime" journalism in Australia, with the website owner claiming the now-deleted outlets were an experiment gone wrong.

items.title: New Adelaide University study finds many purebred dingoes contain some domestic dog genetics
items.summary: A new study into dingo DNA has found many animals previously considered to be purebred actually have domestic dog genetics.

items.title: Meteor lights up skies over NSW
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Footage shows 'fireball' meteor lighting up skies in parts of eastern Australia
items.summary: Colourful streaks across the sky have been spotted across eastern Australia as a "fireball" meteor passed through the atmosphere.

items.title: US-bound plane diverted after passenger from Ebola-hit Congo boards 'in error'
items.summary: The US has introduced new restrictions on travellers from countries exposed to an Ebola outbreak in Africa, requiring them to enter through Washington DC.

items.title: Arsenal coach Mikel Arteta says he cooked barbecue instead of watching game that gave him EPL title
items.summary: Mikel Arteta says he decided to not watch the match that secured Arsenal an historic EPL title, instead deciding to cook a barbecue.

items.title: Sale's premier sporting export Scott Pendlebury almost never played AFL
items.summary: Scott Pendlebury is about to set the record for most AFL matches played. This is the latest milestone in a stellar career that almost never was.

items.title: Guzman Y Gomez soars on US exit, ASX climbs higher — as it happened
items.summary: Australian markets have risen this morning, with Guzman Y Gomez surging on news it has pulled out of the US market.

items.title: Four-metre waves on the way as south-east Queensland braces for beach erosion
items.summary: Large swells driven by a Tasman Sea low are expected to bring hazardous surf and further erosion to already affected Gold Coast beaches over the weekend, before easing early next week.

items.title: Parents of children in NT foster care say concerns around black eyes, bite marks ignored by department
items.summary: The parents of two children in Northern Territory foster care have spoken out about their "heartbreaking" experience with the system, saying concerns their daughter was being abused by her foster family were brushed off by the department.

items.title: What do we know about how changes to family trusts affect farmers?
items.summary: Despite winning a carve-out to changes to family trusts announced in the budget, farmers are still worried about what tax reform means for them.

items.title: Questions raised over demolition of 150-year-old Tasmanian mansion
items.summary: How did a colonial-era mansion once earmarked to be on the state heritage register get bulldozed?

items.title: Cuba accepts offer of $140m in American aid, Rubio says
items.summary: Tensions between the longtime Cold War rivals have hit a new low in recent days after the US indicted the country's 94-year-old former president Raúl Castro.

items.title: Optus fails to warn Coleambally residents about 13-day planned phone outage
items.summary: Optus has apologised after failing to notify a western Riverina town about a 13-day planned outage for service upgrades.

items.title: Algal bloom, fishing restrictions see seafood prices rise as King George Whiting hits $100 per kilo
items.summary: South Australia's peak seafood body says a short supply of some species due to the algal bloom and commercial fishing restrictions has led to slight price increases, as it warns it could take industry five to 10 years to fully recover.

items.title: Cubans describe life under oil blockade and uncertainty over America's next move
items.summary: As power and petrol supplies run out, Cubans tell the ABC about their daily lives. "You can no longer find streets packed with travellers, music and dance," one says. "Only Cuban nationals, still attempting to take some bread home."

items.title: Caravan repairers battling to stay afloat amid fuel crisis impacting customers
items.summary: Normally at this time of year, RVs are hitting the road to head north and escape winter. But the ongoing fuel crisis is forcing travellers to stay closer to home, causing a serious economic downturn for many of the 2,500 businesses in the RV repair industry.

items.title: CVs losing value for hiring as use of artificial intelligence changes industry, recruiters say
items.summary: AI-perfected CVs are becoming the norm as more job applicants and employers use artificial intelligence, but recruiters say the technology could be making hiring trickier.

items.title: Snowy Hydro bosses paid more than $1.2m in bonuses as renewables project costs spiral
items.summary: The renewables project's chief executive, Dennis Barnes, received $323,000 in bonus pay on top of his $1.69 million salary last financial year, while four other senior executives received about $919,000 in "variable pay".

items.title: Treasurer asked to explain productivity impact of tax changes
items.summary: The Productivity Commission was not asked to model how changes to capital gains tax would impact Australia's economy. Business groups say Treasurer Jim Chalmers must explain how the government concluded its tax changes would ultimately be a boon to productivity.

items.title: Iran tightens grip on Strait of Hormuz with new transit regime
items.summary: Iran is asserting unprecedented control over the strategically critical waterway, with ships reportedly facing screening processes, transit approvals and potential Bitcoin-backed insurance requirements.

items.title: Welcome to Robinvale, the town where people are not counted
items.summary: In a community like no other, what happens when people aren't counted? Welcome to Robinvale, where these workers and their families don't exist.

items.title: ASIO cut counterterrorism funding share to post-9/11 low before Bondi attack
items.summary: Australia's intelligence agencies had cut counterterrorism funding to its lowest share this century before the Bondi massacre, despite warnings that a terrorist attack was probable and that antisemitic violence posed an escalating threat to life.

items.title: Safety top priority as drone shows return to Vivid Sydney light festival
items.summary: Dazzling drone shows will return to Sydney's largest art and culture festival for the first time since 2024.

items.title: Harry Connick Jr and Jessica Gethin share a Carnegie Hall moment
items.summary: Harry Connick Jr's new symphony, Elaboratio, is filled with musical tributes to his mother Anita. Orchestra Victoria and Australian conductor Jessica Gethin are bringing those memories to life at Carnegie Hall.

items.title: ABC news quiz: A pumped-up prince, a grinning sculpture, a damaged icon and a stowaway frog
items.summary: You'll be as happy as Prince William when you take this week's quiz, which goes off like a frog in a … bag of lettuce.

items.title: Another group of Australians with IS links leave camp in Syria for Australia
items.summary: The ABC has seen buses carrying the group, which left the al-Roj camp on Thursday afternoon, local time.

items.title: The Mushroom Tapes, Mad Mabel triumph at Australian Book Industry Awards
items.summary: In May 2025, Helen Garner, Chloe Hooper and Sarah Krasnostein, three literary true-crime writers, headed to Erin Patterson's trial. The resulting book just won an Australian Book Industry Award.

items.title: Thursday finance with Daniel Ziffer
items.summary: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

items.title: Israeli ambassador criticises Ben-Gvir's video but calls Gaza flotilla a 'provocation'
items.summary: Israel's ambassador to Australia Hillel Newman has stopped short of apologising to the Global Sumud Flotilla activists detained in Israel who had been taunted by far-right minister Itamar Ben-Gvir.

items.title: 'I've never been so confused': Raiders slam officials, fall to Dolphins in thriller
items.summary: The Raiders huffed and puffed but couldn't overcome the depleted Dolphins, who despite missing six key players through injury and Origin won a fourth-straight NRL game for the first time.

items.title: Pauline Hanson proposes new gas tax and public stake in drilling projects
items.summary: The One Nation leader wants a flat royalty on the production of new gas projects.

items.title: 'Emotional' Hawks kick off Tassie farewell as star Crow injured
items.summary: Blake Hardwick emerges the unlikeliest of heroes for Hawthorn as the Hawks record a nine-point win over Adelaide in Tasmania.

items.title: SpaceX files for largest IPO in history, with potential to make Elon Musk the world's first trillionaire
items.summary: Elon Musk's sprawling space exploration company, which also owns Starlink and xAI, was valued at $US1.25 trillion ($1.75 trillion) earlier this year, meaning Musk could become the world's first trillionaire if the offering goes ahead at that valuation.

items.title: Albanese's budget backlash tour spends little time with Coalition
items.summary: For a group of people who declared they were prepared to tackle a post-budget scare campaign, the government has looked anything but in control this week.

items.title: Isabelle Kelly returns to chase an Origin dynasty 10 years after breakthrough Blues win
items.summary: Ten years after playing in New South Wales's breakthrough victory over Queensland, Isabelle Kelly returns to the Gold Coast on the cusp of launching a Blues Origin dynasty.

items.title: NDIS integrity inquiry hears raft of alarming claims and calls for greater whistleblower protections
items.summary: A raft of alarming evidence has been aired at a parliamentary hearing into the integrity of the NDIS, with calls for the government to strengthen whistleblower protections.

items.title: AFLW's 2026 fixture to feature women's and men's double-headers
items.summary: A series of double-headers involving the women's and men's competitions will take place after the AFLW season begins in August.

items.title: Coles investigates Apple gift card scam after Melbourne artist's viral complaint
items.summary: Coles is looking into a Melbourne mother's complaint about faulty Apple gift cards worth $2,000.

items.title: The Besties say violent podcast clip is 'just one example of what we deal with' as female creators
items.summary: A video circulated across social media of a group of men laughing about how one of them would "beat the f***" out of a group of female content creators.

items.title: Arafura Rare Earths mine green-lit as federal government agrees to buy 500 tonnes of product per year
items.summary: Arafura Rare Earths has announced its flagship project north of Alice Springs will go ahead, decades after it was first flagged as a prospect.

items.title: Jai Arrow's MND diagnosis shines spotlight on debilitating condition that has no cure
items.summary: As another rugby league player is diagnosed with motor neurone disease, we asked experts why so little is known about what causes it.

items.title: Family's story of dementia shines spotlight on Australia's leading cause of death
items.summary: Gwen Knox, from Western Australia, has written a play inspired by her family that she hopes starts important conversations about dementia and truth-telling.

items.title: Israeli minister's taunting of activists no surprise to those following his career
items.summary: Benjamin Netanyahu has condemned a video of his national security minister taunting activists, including Australians, who have been detained. But it's the prime minister who allowed this behaviour to percolate.

items.title: Liberal leader Angus Taylor spruiks gas extraction in Adelaide as SA party opposes fracking proposal
items.summary: After telling an Adelaide oil and gas conference "we need to get busy digging and drilling", Opposition Leader Angus Taylor bats away questions about the SA Liberal Party's resistance to fracking in the state's South East.

items.title: Peak body slams ministers' absence from Aboriginal housing and homelands conference as 'disgraceful'
items.summary: The Northern Territory's peak body for Aboriginal housing says it's "disgraceful" ministers from both levels of government have failed to attend a conference on the housing crisis.

items.title: Rockhampton Plaza Hotel sells for $5.75 million at court-ordered auction
items.summary: After its closure in 2014, the formerly popular hotel became dilapidated and was frequented by squatters. It has now sold at a court-ordered auction for $5.75 million.

items.title: Kylie doco is a human portrait of pop royalty's resilience and reinvention
items.summary: "Our Kylie" gets candid in the three-part Netflix docuseries, grieving over IVF struggles, Michael Hutchence, and revealing she was diagnosed with cancer a second time in 2021.

items.title: Australia backs landmark UN climate change ruling as others try to block it
items.summary: Australia joins 140 other countries in passing a major United Nations resolution backing a landmark legal ruling on climate change, despite efforts by the United States, Russia and Saudi Arabia to sink it.

items.title: Australia's largest diphtheria outbreak prompts $7.2m federal vaccine blitz
items.summary: The federal government has stepped in to address the largest diphtheria outbreak since records began, unveiling a multi-million dollar package for more vaccines and workers to deliver them.

items.title: The deadly 1996 plane downing at the heart of Raúl Castro's indictment
items.summary: The United States has indicted former Cuban president Raúl Castro on murder charges stemming from 1996, reviving one of the lowest points in its tangled history with Cuba.

items.title: McDonald's confirms plan for restaurant in historic Castlemaine
items.summary: McDonald's says it has identified a site in Castlemaine and is exploring the possibility of establishing a restaurant, but it's not being welcomed by some in the town.

items.title: Football Australia posts record losses as plan to axe 20 per cent of staff revealed
items.summary: About 20 per cent of Football Australia staffers face the axe as soccer's governing body posts record losses that doubled last financial year's numbers.

items.title: Earthquakes hit near regional Queensland town twice in four days
items.summary: Experts say this week's seismic activity is unusual, but the region has a pattern of large and clustered earthquakes.

items.title: Five reasons Stephen Colbert is one of the most important satirists in American history
items.summary: The best satirists do more than entertain. They influence public discourse and leave lasting marks on political life.
